explanation:
The natural language requirement specifies that in PCV or PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration and only if no inspiratory pause is set, it should be possible to start a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if requested by the GUI. Based on the available atomic propositions, the logical condition should state: whenever (globally) the ventilator is in PCVMode or PSVMode, and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and the inspiratory pause is not set (i.e., !inspiratoryPauseButton), and the Recruitment Maneuver is requested (RMButton), then it is possible to initiate RM (Recruitment Maneuver). In LTL, 'it shall be possible to initiate RM' typically means that after these conditions, RM can be activated (i.e., eventually RM will be true if all those preconditions hold) — so we use the 'eventually' operator (F) to capture this possible future activation. Also, we need to encode the exclusion of inspiratory pause (no inspiratoryPauseButton active). 

Therefore, for every time the following condition is true: (in PCV or PSV mode) & inspiratory phase ends & inspiratory pause is not set & RM is requested by the GUI, eventually RM should become true. This is captured as G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM).
output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM)

explanation:
The natural language requirement states that in both PCV and PSV modes, at the end of an inspiration and if an inspiratory pause is not required, it should be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if required by the GUI. To translate this into LTL using the available atomic propositions, we note the following:

- "in PCV and PSV mode": both PCVMode and PSVMode quantum, so PCVMode | PSVMode is true when either mode is active.
- "at the end of inspiration": inspiratoryPhaseEnd identifies this.
- "if inspiratory pause is not required": !inspiratoryPauseButton (button not engaged/set by GUI).
- "RM can occur if required by GUI": RMButton is pressed, so RMButton.
- "it shall be possible to initiate RM": we require that, whenever PCVMode or PSVMode is active, AND inspiratoryPhaseEnd holds AND inspiratoryPauseButton is not set AND RMButton is set, then it is possible to activate RM, i.e., RM should occur (possibly after some delay or immediately, but at least eventually).

Thus the LTL captures the stated behavior by specifying that whenever all preconditions are satisfied, eventually RM will be activated. This triggers a guarantee (G):

G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton ) -> F RM )

This ensures completeness and a direct mapping of the NL requirements.

Prior approaches might either miss the combined mode restrictions, wrongly model the timing (e.g., requiring RM immediately at inspiratoryPhaseEnd), or miss the RMButton requirement. This synthesis covers all necessary conjunctions and the possibility guarantee.
output_LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton ) -> F RM )

explanation: The requirement is that, when in PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and if the inspiratory pause is NOT required, and if the GUI requests it, it must be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM). The translation identifies (1) PCVMode or PSVMode as being active, (2) inspiratoryPhaseEnd as the end of inspiration, (3) inspiratoryPauseButton as an indication of requested pause (so NOT inspiratoryPauseButton means pause not required/requested), and (4) RMButton as the GUI's request for RM. The formula says globally: if (PCVMode or PSVMode), AND it is the end of inspiration, AND inspiratory pause is NOT required, AND GUI has requested RM, then it should be possible to activate RM (meaning RM can be true in the next state). This captures the essence that after inspiration, if pause is not required, and GUI requests, RM should be possible to initiate. Previous formulations might omit the temporal ordering (trigger right after inspiration ends), or might not correctly require all these conditions simultaneously.
output_LTL: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> XF RM)

explanation:
The requirement states that in PCV and PSV modes, at the end of an inspiration (and when inspiratory pause is not required), it must be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if required by the GUI. In terms of atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' denote the ventilation modes, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' denotes the end of inspiration, 'inspiratoryPauseButton' denotes whether an inspiratory pause is set (by the GUI), and 'RMButton' denotes whether the GUI requests recruitment maneuver. The property must allow that whenever the system is in PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, if the inspiratory pause is not requested (i.e., 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is NOT active), then, if the GUI requests RM (i.e., 'RMButton'), it is possible to activate RM ('RM' can become true). 

The LTL formula globally checks that at every instant, after an inspiratory phase end in PCV or PSV mode, and if the inspiratory pause button is not active, if the GUI requests RM, then eventually RM can be made true. Numerically, we cannot specify temporal windows beyond 'eventually', but this suffices for possibility. Previous/incorrect translations might force RM to become true always or under wrong circumstances (e.g., ignoring the inspiratoryPauseButton, or not distinguishing modes). This translation correctly captures the conditional possibility requirement:

Globally, whenever we are in PCV or PSV mode, at end of inspiration, and inspiratory pause is not set, if RM is requested, then eventually RM is possible:
output_LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton ) -> F RM )

explanation: The requirement describes that in PCV or PSV modes, at the end of inspiration and if inspiratory pause is NOT required, it should be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if the GUI requires it. In LTL with the given atomic propositions, we capture 'in PCV or PSV mode' as (PCVMode | PSVMode), 'at end of inspiration' as inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and 'inspiratory pause is not required' as !(inspiratoryPauseButton). To initiate RM, we require RMButton from the GUI. Thus, the property says: whenever we are (in PCV or PSV mode) and inspiratory phase ends and inspiratory pause is not required and GUI sets RMButton, then RM must be initiated (active) in the next state. The translation expresses this with: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> X RM). Previous LTL properties might overlook the need to check for both mode, require inspiratory phase end, and the absence of inspiratory pause button, as well as aligning the RM action with the GUI's RMButton signal.
output_LTL: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> X RM)

explanation: The input natural language asks that in PCV and PSV mode, at the end of inspiration and as long as an inspiratory pause is NOT required, if the GUI requires it (i.e., Recruitment Maneuver button pressed), it shall be possible to initiate the lung recruitment maneuver (RM). In LTL, we capture this by stating: 'Whenever it is the end of the inspiratory phase, AND we are in PCV or PSV mode, AND inspiratory pause is NOT set by the GUI, AND the RMButton is set by the GUI, THEN it is possible to initiate RM.' This can be translated as: Globally, if (inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND (PCVMode OR PSVMode) AND NOT inspiratoryPauseButton AND RMButton), then eventually RM.' Note: The LTL expresses permission possibility by requiring that if the triggering condition holds, RM must become true eventually. Previous LTLs might not capture the explicit temporal relationship or the 'possible to initiate' constraint, or neglect the requirements on the modes, the end of inspiration, or the NOT inspiratory pause condition.

The requirement specifies that in PCV or PSV modes, at the end of inspiration, if an inspiratory pause is not required, and if the GUI requests it, it should be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM). This is interpreted as: whenever we reach the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND we are in PCVMode OR PSVMode, AND there is NOT an inspiratory pause set by the GUI (¬inspiratoryPauseButton), AND RM is requested by the GUI (RMButton), then it should be possible for the Recruitment Maneuver to be initiated (i.e., RM should become true). Since LTL does not reason about 'possibility', we capture this as a safety property: whenever the conditions are true, if the GUI requires RM (by pressing RMButton), then RM should be activated. The property captures the logic as a response to these conditions at every step globally. More complex deontic logic expressing the capability directly is not expressible in standard LTL, but this captures the intended trigger closely.

The requirement states: In PCV and PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment maneuver if required by the GUI. 

To capture this in LTL, we need the following:
1. The system is in PCV or PSV mode (atomic propositions: PCVMode | PSVMode).
2. The end of inspiration is indicated (inspiratoryPhaseEnd).
3. Inspiratory pause is not required (the negative of inspiratoryPauseButton).
4. The GUI requests RM (RMButton).
5. It should be possible to initiate RM (the RM must be able to become true in the next state).

LTL cannot express 'possibility' directly (as in CTL's EF operator), but we often encode this by requiring that, whenever the above conjunction is true, then it must become possible that RM is set in the next state. As a basic safety check, we must ensure that if the system is in PCV or PSV, and inspiratory phase ends, and no inspiratoryPause is set, and the GUI requests RM, then RM is true in the next state.

This can be written as: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> X RM)

The key is that we do not use any numerical comparison operators and capture the conditional structure of the requirement. This LTL property is the most straightforward encoding given the available atomic propositions and the constraints of LTL's expressiveness.
output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> X RM)

explanation: The property describes that in both PCV and PSV modes, at the moment an inspiration ends, if an inspiratory pause is not required (that is, the inspiratory pause button has not been pressed), and if a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is required by the GUI (simulated here by RMButton being true at that time), then it must be possible to initiate an RM (represented by RM being true). This is captured by stating that, globally, whenever (PCVMode OR PSVMode) AND inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND NOT inspiratoryPauseButton AND RMButton holds, then eventually RM will be true right after (i.e., in the next state). We use XRM to indicate that RM should become active in the next state. This LTL captures the conditional initiation of RM at inspiration end, covering all the described requirements tied to mode, state, and GUI request. Previous indirect encodings might miss the simultaneity or specific modal triggers mentioned in the statement.
output_LTL: G (((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> X RM)

explanation:
The input requires that in PCV or PSV modes, when an inspiration ends and there is no inspiratory pause, it shall be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if it is requested by the GUI. To encode this in LTL:

- "PCV and PSV mode" can be expressed as PCVMode | PSVMode.
- "at the end of an inspiration" is captured by inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
- "if inspiratory pause is not required" means inspiratoryPauseButton is not set by the GUI (negation of inspiratoryPauseButton).
- "it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure...if it is required by the GUI" can be formalized as: if both conditions above are true and RMButton is set (i.e., the GUI requests it), then RM shall (eventually) be activated from this point.

The property states: Globally, whenever the mode is PCV or PSV, and inspiration ends, and the inspiratoryPauseButton is not set, if the GUI requests RM (RMButton), then RM is eventually triggered. Prior counterparts may have missed the order, negation of pause, or the GUI input dependence.

LTL: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> F RM)
output_LTL: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> F RM)
